Clockwork Watchman Greaves  
 


Slot: LEGS
Charges: 10
AC: 13
This item can be used in tradeskills.
Effect: Grease Injection (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 4.0) at Level 20
WT: 5.6 Size: LARGE
Class: WAR CLR PAL SHD
Race: GNM
Clockwork Watchman Greaves

Slot: LEGS
Charges: 10
AC: 13
This item can be used in tradeskills.
Effect: Grease Injection (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 4.0) at Level 20
WT: 5.6 Size: LARGE
Class: WAR CLR PAL SHD
Race: GNM

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Velious Plate 2
Tint:

Page Updated:Tue Mar 13th, 2018

Average Price: No Data Pricing Data...
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 1
[Recipes | Comments ]

    • Class: ALL
    Expansion: Not Set

Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
Post Comment
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 12 2002 at 4:21 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) YOU GUYS ARE GETTING TWO DIFFERENT BOOTS MIXED UP. WATCHMANS BOOTS CAST SOW WHILE CLOCKWORK WATCHMANS BOOTS IS SOWLIKE SPELL ON GREAVES AND RECHARGED WITH MANA 5 BATTERY WHICH GIVES IT 10 CHARGES OF SOWLIKE SPELL WHERE WATCHMANS BOOTS I DONT KNOW IF YOU CAN RECHARGE.
#Anonymous, Posted: May 23 2001 at 4:30 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) where do you buy tboots? ^_^
RE: where?!
QuoteReply
# Nov 10 2002 at 2:24 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592758645020
32 posts
Tboots are sold by Marfen Binkdirple in SolA. You have to make yer way into the Clockwork Gnome area and he is in the first door on the left as you enter the "fort". They are about 5.25kpp for my 56 bard 135ish CHA.

Edited, Sun Nov 10 14:28:15 2002
▲ Page top
Using
QuoteReply
# May 23 2001 at 12:43 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592248183590
1 post
My warrior has been using these for little over 2 months now. They where a nice item before the Tboots, and still are since I didn't pay anything for these.

I also have the Vambraces, Helm, and Gauntlets. Over the last few months of using these items and constantly recharging them, I have not failed any attempts to recharge them yet. The batteries cost about 55pp each, so cost gets a bit high with constant use of my set of armor, since I'm only lv40.

As for what they look like. They look different on both female and male gnomes. My warrior is a female. They are an off white, with blue waist area and gems placed in different areas. A very unique look. If you can't find a PC with a full set anywhere to look at, the Minister of Bertoxxulous on the 2nd floor of hate wears a full set. Can't wait to finish my set.

P.S. I really love the gauntlets effect.
▲ Page top
charges
QuoteReply
# May 23 2001 at 11:33 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Don't neglect that these pants only have 10 charges of the effect. That being the case, you're better off buying sow potions and keeping these for the AC.
BTW, anyone know what the graphic on these is? Would be cool if a full suit of clockwork stuff made you look like a clockwork NPC.
▲ Page top
RE: charges
QuoteReply
# Aug 08 2002 at 8:43 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591780131652
View User Forum
101 posts
Why would sow potions be better? Aren't these rechargable with a mana battery? 55pp for a mana battery opposed to 100pp for a 10 dose of sow...hmmmmm
▲ Page top
Other parts
QuoteReply
# May 23 2001 at 8:01 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
There is at least klockwork vambraces, they had a haste effect. Not sure of the percent tho
▲ Page top
The effect
QuoteReply
# May 23 2001 at 1:46 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591750246078
Scholar
312 posts
The effect on these is similar to Jboots, with a different message. It's roughly the same speed.

haste is from the arms. They rock. I've read of the effects, and so far every warrior that uses them love them, except for the fact that they're charged, not unlimited.

All Watchman Clock armor can be recharged by replacing the mana battery. It's supposedly trivial at level 1, so it's HIGHLY unlikly you'd fail, and even if you do, I believe that you get the greeves back.
▲ Page top
Watchmanboots
QuoteReply
# May 23 2001 at 12:22 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591751692455
34 posts
watchman boots had one charge of sow(cheap)
▲ Page top
Dont knock this..
QuoteReply
# May 22 2001 at 11:44 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248574855
18 posts
But these babies go for around 10kpp. Now many of you will simply want to go and buy travelers boots, but your missing out on a really nice item here that took ALOT of work to make.

Pretty Sweet
QuoteReply
# May 22 2001 at 10:21 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Decent AC for low-mid lvls. effect is sweet too in my opinion. How much these go for and are there other pieces that can be made? Oh, and anyone know what they look like?

effect
QuoteReply
# May 22 2001 at 6:01 PM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Grease Injection
Increase Movement by 40% (L1)


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Classes: None

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Skill: Instantaneous
Casting Area Restriction: Outdoor
Allowable Targets: Self

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Resistance Check: None

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Spell Duration: 180 ticks (18.0 minutes)
Duration Formula: 3

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Spell cast on you: An injection of heated grease speeds the movement of your armor.'s armor is sped up by an injection of heated grease.
Spell cast on someone: Soandso's armor is sped up by an injection of heated grease.
Spell fades: Your armor slows.
